body.notes: Participation in IETF meetings constitutes agreement to the Note Well, which permits recording, transcription, and public archival of proceedings. See also BCP 78 (RFC 5378) and BCP 79 (RFC 8179) for IPR policies.

meta.title: Grant Negotiation and Authorization Protocol Resource Server Connections

transcript.text: e capology that they're using it's called is me e that and Echo mind I think going okay yeah I love the starting slide that's so cute and did yeah so welcome everyone we actually so I'm here on chefer we have more Koalas in the room than chairs H Leaf couldn't make it to Australia or even remotely so I'll be sharing a meeting today okay it's already midweek if you haven't seen the note well yet please take a long look at the note well it applies to everything that we do at the ITF and at this meeting um we will have a few starting words from me H followed by Justin who will describe the latest uh status of the two gnap documents and then if time remain or time should remain for any other business otherwise we are very likely to end early um so speaking of status uh the core the Gap core protocol has been approved by the isg for publication that's a very big uh achievement for us thank you to everyone who who was in involved and this only happened last week I believe so really really recently um RS protocol um post chairs uh read it uh there there was another revision we opened last call and there was absolute crickets from the mailing list no review during a working group last call H we will be discussing so the chairs and the authors will be discussing this uh document with the ads or with the incoming ad de Kuli um soon after this ITF meeting ER most likely we will wrap up this working group immediately and since the core protocol is already in the RFC at the toyou and we don't seem to have enough energy to drive the RS protocol through the working group uh but this is all very tentative pending on ER on the ad's approach to this um yeah one last thing that I neglect neglected to say uh there were some very late small minor changes to the core protocol and therefore Justin published it uh earlier today uh so that's the lat latest version that's the version that's going into uh the RFC editor right now and with that I'll pass it to Justin I don't know if this is going to work but yeah not you could try try the remot TR the next slide thing yeah here I hit the button to reides okay and this h no hover over me and then hit the yes it kind of disappears okay cool that should work all right so hi everybody I'm Justin richer um as yurin mentioned uh we've got two gra drafts in the working group and I'm going to go through kind of the changes that we've had since last time and um where we're going to go from here um so uh as is tradition uh in the slide deck you can get the diffs between the drafts from the last time as of this morning this is actually now out of date because there is now a uh a-20 that does one small change which I'll talk about in a moment um I didn't have a chance to uh update uh these these slides but the change is minor um craft went through uh a handful of changes vast majority of them were editorial based on iesg feedback clarification of Concepts uh that kinds of things once again I didn't do the a registrations right uh every single time I write an RFC I seem to learn some new thing that I get wrong um but uh those seem to be good uh now uh this latest one was about media requests so that's up there there um oh and a lot of clarification of you know the languages the figures like what figur is tied to which text and which examples tied to which um you know sort of surrounding pros and that thing we did actually add a couple of security considerations for things that got uh that came up in the isg review um there were some mtls related bits uh in there encourage you to go look at the diffs it's all you know additive explanatory content there were there was one syntactical change and one additional normative change that I want to cover uh the syntactical change is that we changed the Jose token types for for the Jose key bindings so they are now uh instead of gnap binding plus jwsd they are now gnap binding Das jwsd and that goes across all four different uh types that are in there because there's gnap rotation and gnap binding and then there's also J jwsd and jws versions of both of those so if you have an implementation of gnap and you are and you are using the Jose based key binding mechanisms then this is a syntax change that you need to make in order to be uh fully compliant the reasoning behind this is that that plus carries a lot of semantic meeting in the media type and there was uh resistance of registering new um extensions for jws and jwsd uh from folks in the Jose Community for the P purposes of ganap we were just trying to be good CI citizens and using the structures that we thought were going to be available there um and turns out those structures weren't there and it really is not our place to try and invent them within the ganap protocol we just really needed a way to signal that there are different message types and different container types and so we just have four distinct media types uh that are being registered for those things a lot of the Gap implementations that uh I have personally worked with you HTTP message signatures which is uh not affected by this change so if you're doing that or mtls this doesn't affect you at all but if you are doing the Jose stuff then uh you should change your implementations and change that plus to a minus and then you will be in compliance uh the other normative change is actually on a uh recommended um and that's the number of characters in user codes uh it was pointed out that we didn't have a recommended lower Bound for that we just had a recommended upper Bound for usability sake um and based on uh some fairly quick discussion we um settled around a uh a recommended range of six to eight characters for user-facing codes this is just a recommendation it is not a requirement you're still allowed to make longer codes and if you really want to Shorter codes as well um but there's already a lot of discussion about the nature of those codes what characters you should pick uh how you communicate them how you Target them to your expected users how they're supposed to carry them uh through the system all of that kind of stuff um a lot of that we were able to uh sort of import from uh other documents like the ooth device flow and um and align all of that kind of stuff together uh so again that was a normative change but functionally not really a requirement change uh since it is is advice and as of today it is uh officially in the RFC editor queue for publication with those changes applied um and uh so yeah the uh this is awesome uh it's been a lot of work that's gone into this uh from a lot of people over the last roughly four years uh from uh from chartering to today and uh ganap is a really big document um I was looking over our Charter and Milestones earlier and the core document actually covers the vast majority of the Milestones um in a single cohesive protocol document uh which wasn't necessarily what we thought was going to happen when we started pulling things together but that's that's what it is so very exciting to see that moving forward the RS draft uh the editors did several rounds of cleanup um all culminating in the uh in the current draft that was put out to the working group uh again we added registrations uh if experience tells me anything those are probably wrong in some way that I don't know about yet um importantly we added error responses based on uh how core does things uh we added an acknowledgement section couple of little bits of cleanup no real major changes uh to the document um and then requested working group last call um so we got zero response to working group last call as yarin mentioned none no positive like yes this is fine and I read it no I have a problem with this none at all so we are now trying to figure out what exactly to do with the RS draft and how we're going to do that um if anybody has input into that process or wants to volunteer right now to do a last call review of the RS draft now's a great time to bring that up um but right now we're trying to figure out what the next steps are for that what I would like to see uh as the editor is the RS call to go to uh to the isg and um it is a much much simpler document when compared to core much much shorter and it also borrows and adapts a lot of the structures and syntaxes and pieces from the core do document for its specific focus and so with that in mind I think it's worth going that but that is my personal opinion as a participant um regardless of what happens to the RS draft um the editors think it's time to close the working group and um so hopefully we can uh we can ship this one last document out the door and then uh we do not believe the editors do not believe that we will need a meeting to discuss things in Vancouver and I agree and that's that's it for my slides so very very quick update a lot of stuff that's been happening has been already discussed on the list and and stuff is in motion so thank you Justin any comments any questions anybody um who wishes to support uh the publication of the ARs draft in any way Peter uh yes I just wanted to ask uh given the The Crickets seen in the working group last call are there imple ation of the RS that draft that are being used I I guess I'm a little confused why I mean I understand this group has been relatively small but is nobody interested in this have you have you been turning out pages to the uh to the void um so from my experience uh there are implementations of different pieces of the RS draft uh it the RS draft is not a single cohesive protocol uh it's more a set of things that resource servers can optionally use to sort of enhance their connection to the as whereas ganap core it has a whole lot of things you don't have to implement all of them but you're going to be doing some part of that single protocol to implement Gap with the RS draft I know some people have implemented introspection uh some people are using jot formatted tokens which are described in that draft as well um I don't know of anybody that's using the discovery portion we tried to reach out to get feedback on that uh that was an issue that yarn had filed about the RS facing Discovery and we didn't really get a lot of feedback from the implementation communities um so I don't know of anybody including myself who has implemented every single thing in that end to endend in a single cohesive system okay so it would be hard to say that we had interoperable implementations and lots of experience that that's that is correct yes okay thank you y that that said though I will I will also add that the RS draft deliberately reuses pieces from core and tries to tries really hard to not invent things um when it doesn't need to but the statement still definitely stands okay and with that we bring our meeting to a close as Justin mentioned we we will most likely not meet in Vancouver um but this remains to be seen thank you everyone all right thank you all there it is than cute is there such a thing uh yeah because I know that they can turn it off I don't know if anybody from Meo is listening here but uho is actually on the oh they're on the chat aren't they yeah Le it'll stop the recording okay that's that's awesome thank you you all right I just dropped in the chat okay
